questions.question:
Consider a complete graph \( \text{G} \) with \( 4 \) vertices. The graph \( \text{G} \) has _______ spanning trees.



Graph Theory: NIELIT Scientific Assistant June 2025 | Question: 57

The interval in which the function \( f(x)=\sin x+\cos x, 0 \leq x \leq 2 \pi \) is strictly increasing or strictly decreasing is:



Calculus: NIELIT Scientific Assistant June 2025 | Question: 56
options: Strictly increasing for \( x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \cup\left(\frac{5 \pi}{4}, 2 \pi\right] \) and strictly decreasing for \( x \in\left(\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}\right) \)
Strictly increasing for \( x \in[0, \pi) \) and strictly decreasing for \( x \in(\pi, 2 \pi) \)
Strictly increasing for \( x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right] \cap\left(\frac{5 \pi}{4}, 2 \pi\right] \) and strictly decreasing for \( x \in\left(\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}\right) \)
Strictly decreasing for \( x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \cup\left[\frac{5 \pi}{4}, 2 \pi\right] \) and strictly increasing for \( x \in\left[\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}\right] \)
questions.correct_answer: Strictly increasing for \( x \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \cup\left(\frac{5 \pi}{4}, 2 \pi\right] \) and strictly decreasing for \( x \in\left(\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}\right) \)

questions.correct_answer: A \( \text{DFA} \) has a single state transition for each symbol, while an \( \text{NFA} \) can have multiple transitions.
